title: Mode Catalog
description: Game modes expressed as DATA per ADR-0006 Decision 1 — many distinct original modes are data rows, never hardcoded branches (DDTank's 40+ eRoomType enum that switched stat scaling and damage-formula SHAPE at Player.Reset/MakeDamage is deliberately NOT replicated as a C# switch; see docs/ddtank-source-map.md section 2.4). A 'room' in DDTank conflated the runtime lobby with the ruleset its room-type implied; here the ruleset is the MODE, and a room (the future /server lobby — matchmaking/presence, OUT of scope) merely selects a mode by id. Loaded by Sim.Content.ModeCatalog.FromJson; hosts read the file, /sim parses the text. The match engine never consumes a mode directly: the pure Sim.Match.ModeSetup mapper splits each mode into the engine's existing provenance-free primitives — MatchOptions (friendlyFire/selfDamage), CombatRules (folding in modeMultiplier, the #2 ModeMultiplier seam), and MatchModeRules (winCondition/maxTurns/turnOrder). SCOPE (system #5): two shipped modes (elimination = last-team-standing, the data twin of pre-#5 behavior, pinned to ModeDefinition.Default by a drift-lock test; attrition_timed = turn-limit tiebreak). DEFERRED: matchmaking/lobbies/networking (/server), per-mode item/equipment availability, economy rewards per room (#7), progression gating of modes (#6), mode-specific maps/terrain, dynamic per-turn environment (ADR-0006 Decision 2), the FFA MatchOutcome rename (WinningTeamId already carries the N-team winner), a second turn-order policy (ADR-0004 agility seam), and further win conditions (hold-a-zone, survive-N-turns, defeat-a-boss-target) which extend WinConditionKind + one evaluator case.

id.description: Stable lookup key. Must be unique within the catalog. A room selects a mode by this id.

displayName.description: Display-only label. Has no rule effect.

teamSizes.description: Intended team structure: array length is the team count, each entry that team's roster size ([1,1]=1v1, [2,2]=2v2, [1,1,1,1]=FFA-4). EMPTY = unconstrained (any roster accepted; the default mode uses this). Validate-only via ModeDefinition.AcceptsRoster — roster construction is matchmaking and stays in the host.

modeMultiplier.description: Damage scalar folded into CombatRules.ModeMultiplier (1.0 = no adjustment). The data form of DDTank's per-room damage divisor (e.g. Match room /3) reduced to a scalar in #2 — not per-room formula-shape switching. Anti-inflation (ADR-0006): keep near 1.0.

friendlyFire.description: Maps to MatchOptions.FriendlyFire — allies in the blast take damage when true.

selfDamage.description: Maps to MatchOptions.SelfDamage — the shooter is hit by its own blast when true. Independent of friendlyFire (ADR-0004).

turnOrder.description: Selects the turn-order policy. RoundRobin only today; the data-driven selection seam (the ADR-0004 agility policy lands as a new value + one mapping case).

maxTurns.description: Per-mode turn cap. Must be <= SimConstants.MaxTurnsPerMatch (200), the engine safety ceiling against infinite loops. The default mode uses 200.

winCondition.description: Discriminated union as data: a kind plus the params that kind needs. The match controller evaluates it each turn through the pure Sim.Match.WinConditionEvaluator — the ONLY switch keyed by condition KIND, never by mode id, so many modes reuse a few evaluators. A future kind adds its own params here.

kind.description: LastTeamStanding: last team with a living combatant wins (pre-#5 behavior; also expresses defeat-a-boss-target with the boss as its own team id). TurnLimitTiebreak: same, but the turn cap with >=2 teams alive is decided by 'tiebreak' instead of a no-result.

tiebreak.description: Required for TurnLimitTiebreak, forbidden for LastTeamStanding. The metric that picks the winner at the turn cap (argmax over alive teams; a tie is a Draw).
